description: Use this API to return an array of real values for subsystem zero sequence mutual branches. This API routine was first introduced in release 33.12.0 and 34.0.2. ierr, rarray = azmutreal(sid, owner, ties, flag, brns, string)
python_syntax: ierr, rarray = azmutreal(sid, owner, ties, flag, brns, string)

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: Defines the bus subsystem to be used. (input; -1 by default).

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: A flag indicating owner usage if ownership is a subsystem selection criterion (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: ties
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem branches to include (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: flag
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem branches to include. (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: brns
parameters.description: A flag indicating which subsystem branches to include (ignored if sid is negative). (input; 1 by default).

parameters.name: string
parameters.description: An array of NSTR elements specifying NSTR of the following strings indicating the branch zero sequence mutual quantities desired. (input; no default allowed).

return_values.name: rarray
return_values.description: An array of NSTR*DIM elements specifying array of at least NSTR columns, each of which contains DIM elements. (output).

sid.description: to instruct the API to assume a subsystem containing all buses in the working case.
valid bus subsystem identifier. Valid subsystem identifiers range from 0 to 11. Subsystem SID must have been previously defined.

ties.description: for both interior subsystem branches and tie branches.

flag.description: for both mutually coupled branches must be in-service.

brns.description: for at least one mutually coupled branch in subsystem.
for both mutually coupled branches in subsystem.

error_codes.description: DIM, and hence the size of RARRAY, is not large enough.
